ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the employee */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the employee */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the employee */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the employee */,
  "address" TEXT /* Residential address of the employee */,
  "emergency_contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the emergency contact */,
  "emergency_contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the emergency contact */,
  "job_title_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job title */,
  "started_on" TEXT /* Date the employee started working */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the employee */,
  "status_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employment status */,
  "hire_date" TEXT /* Date the employee was hired */,
  "termination_date" TEXT /* Date the employee was terminated (if applicable) */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee's manager */,
  "salary" TEXT /* Salary of the employee */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the employee belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("status_id") REFERENCES employment_status ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES managers ("manager_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_job_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_title_id") REFERENCES job_titles ("job_title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_titles" (
  "job_title_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job title */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Name of the job title */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department to which the job title belongs */,
  "job_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the job title */,
  "job_level" TEXT /* Level of the job title (e.g., Entry, Mid, Senior) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_title_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_titles_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employment_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employment status */,
  "status" TEXT /* Name of the employment status (e.g., Active, Suspended) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the employment status */,
  "effective_date" TEXT /* Date the status change became effective */,
  "reason_for_status_change" TEXT /* Reason for the status change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "department_description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "managers" (
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each manager */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee who is a manager */,
  "manager_start_date" TEXT /* Date the employee became a manager */,
  "manager_end_date" TEXT /* Date the employee ceased to be a manager (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("manager_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_managers_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log entry */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee whose record was changed */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed (e.g., Create, Update, Delete) */,
  "action_date" TEXT /* Date and time the action was performed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "details" TEXT /* Details of the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES user_access ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., HR, Manager, Admin) */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g., Read, Write, Admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
